,"type":"uint256[]"}],"stateMutability":"payable","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"This contract allow interacting with ERC4626 Pools (which contain wrapped ERC4626 tokens) using only underlying standard tokens. For instance, with `addLiquidityUnbalancedToERC4626Pool` it is possible to add liquidity to an ERC4626 Pool with [waDAI, waUSDC], using only DAI, only USDC, or an arbitrary amount of both. If the ERC4626 buffers in the Vault have liquidity, these will be used to avoid wrapping/unwrapping through the wrapped token interface, saving gas. For instance, adding only DAI to the pool above (and assuming a waDAI buffer with enough liquidity), would pull in the DAI from the user, swap it for waDAI in the internal Vault buffer, and deposit the waDAI into the ERC4626 pool: 1) without having to do any expensive ERC4626 wrapping operations; and 2) without requiring the user to construct a batch operation containing the buffer swap.","errors":{"DuplicateTokenIn(address)":[{"details":"Note that it's technically possible to have duplicate tokens with 0 amounts, as those are ignored.","params":{"duplicateToken":"The address of the duplicate token"}}],"WrongTokensOut(address[],address[])":[{"params":{"actualTokensOut":"The set of tokens derived from pool traversal","expectedTokensOut":"The set of tokens supplied by the user"}}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"addLiquidityProportionalToERC4626Pool(address,bool[],uint256[],uint256,bool,bytes)":{"details":"An \"ERC4626 pool\" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI). Ensure that any buffers associated with the wrapped tokens in the ERC4626 pool have been initialized before initializing or adding liquidity to the \"parent\" pool, and also make sure limits are set properly.","params":{"exactBptAmountOut":"Exact amount of pool tokens to be received","maxAmountsIn":"Maximum amounts of underlying/wrapped tokens in, sorted in token registration order","pool":"Address of the liquidity pool","userData":"Additional (optional) data required for adding liquidity","wethIsEth":"If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH","wrapUnderlying":"Flags indicating whether the corresponding token should be wrapped or used as an ERC20"},"returns":{"amountsIn":"Actual amounts of tokens added to the pool"}},"addLiquidityUnbalancedNestedPool(address,address[],uint256[],address[],uint256,bool,bytes)":{"details":"A nested pool is one in which one or more tokens are BPTs from another pool (child pool). Since there are multiple pools involved, the token order is not well-defined, and must be specified by the caller. If the parent or nested pools contain ERC4626 tokens that appear in the `tokensToWrap` list, they will be wrapped and their underlying tokens pulled as input, and expected to appear in `tokensIn`. Otherwise, they will be treated as regular tokens. NB: Pools with \"overlapping\" tokens (i.e., both the parent and a child pool contain one or more of the tokens in `tokensIn`), are not supported! The gas cost to explicitly detect this rare edge case would be prohibitive, so behavior in this case is undefined.","params":{"exactAmountsIn":"An array with the amountIn of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensIn","minBptAmountOut":"Expected minimum amount of parent pool tokens to receive","parentPool":"The address of the parent pool (which contains BPTs of other pools)","tokensIn":"An array with all tokens from the child pools, and all non-BPT parent tokens, in arbitrary order","tokensToWrap":"A list of ERC4626 tokens which should be wrapped if encountered during pool traversal","userData":"Additional (optional) data required for the operation","wethIsEth":"If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH"},"returns":{"bptAmountOut":"The actual amount of parent pool tokens received"}},"addLiquidityUnbalancedToERC4626Pool(address,bool[],uint256[],uint256,bool,bytes)":{"details":"An \"ERC4626 pool\" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI). addLiquidityProportionalToERC4626Pool(ad.details: An "ERC4626 pool" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI). Ensure that any buffers associated with the wrapped tokens in the ERC4626 pool have been initialized before initializing or adding liquidity to the "parent" pool, and also make sure limits are set properly.

params.exactBptAmountOut: Exact amount of pool tokens to be received
params.maxAmountsIn: Maximum amounts of underlying/wrapped tokens in, sorted in token registration order
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for adding liquidity
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH
params.wrapUnderlying: Flags indicating whether the corresponding token should be wrapped or used as an ERC20

returns.amountsIn: Actual amounts of tokens added to the pool

addLiquidityUnbalancedNestedPool(address.details: A nested pool is one in which one or more tokens are BPTs from another pool (child pool). Since there are multiple pools involved, the token order is not well-defined, and must be specified by the caller. If the parent or nested pools contain ERC4626 tokens that appear in the `tokensToWrap` list, they will be wrapped and their underlying tokens pulled as input, and expected to appear in `tokensIn`. Otherwise, they will be treated as regular tokens. NB: Pools with "overlapping" tokens (i.e., both the parent and a child pool contain one or more of the tokens in `tokensIn`), are not supported! The gas cost to explicitly detect this rare edge case would be prohibitive, so behavior in this case is undefined.

params.exactAmountsIn: An array with the amountIn of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensIn
params.minBptAmountOut: Expected minimum amount of parent pool tokens to receive
params.parentPool: The address of the parent pool (which contains BPTs of other pools)
params.tokensIn: An array with all tokens from the child pools, and all non-BPT parent tokens, in arbitrary order
params.tokensToWrap: A list of ERC4626 tokens which should be wrapped if encountered during pool traversal
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the operation
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH

returns.bptAmountOut: The actual amount of parent pool tokens received

addLiquidityUnbalancedToERC4626Pool(addr.details: An "ERC4626 pool" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI). Ensure that any buffers associated with the wrapped tokens in the ERC4626 pool have been initialized before initializing or adding liquidity to the "parent" pool, and also make sure limits are set properly.

params.exactAmountsIn: Exact amounts of underlying/wrapped tokens in, sorted in token registration order
params.minBptAmountOut: Minimum amount of pool tokens to be received
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for adding liquidity
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH
params.wrapUnderlying: Flags indicating whether the corresponding token should be wrapped or used as an ERC20

returns.bptAmountOut: Actual amount of pool tokens received

queryAddLiquidityProportionalToERC4626Po.details: An "ERC4626 pool" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI).

params.exactBptAmountOut: Exact amount of pool tokens to be received
params.sender: The sender passed to the operation. It can influence results (e.g., with user-dependent hooks)
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the query
params.wrapUnderlying: Flags indicating whether the corresponding token should be wrapped or used as an ERC20

returns.amountsIn: Expected amounts of tokens added to the pool

params.exactAmountsIn: An array with the amountIn of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensIn
params.parentPool: The address of the parent pool (which contains BPTs of other pools)
params.sender: The sender passed to the operation. It can influence results (e.g., with user-dependent hooks)
params.tokensIn: An array with all tokens from the child pools, and all non-BPT parent tokens, in arbitrary order
params.tokensToWrap: A list of ERC4626 tokens which should be wrapped if encountered during pool traversal
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the operation

returns.bptAmountOut: The actual amount of parent pool tokens received

queryAddLiquidityUnbalancedToERC4626Pool.details: An "ERC4626 pool" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI).

params.exactAmountsIn: Exact amounts of underlying/wrapped tokens in, sorted in token registration order
params.sender: The sender passed to the operation. It can influence results (e.g., with user-dependent hooks)
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the query
params.wrapUnderlying: Flags indicating whether the corresponding token should be wrapped or used as an ERC20

returns.bptAmountOut: Expected amount of pool tokens to receive

queryRemoveLiquidityProportionalFromERC4.details: An "ERC4626 pool" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI).

params.exactBptAmountIn: Exact amount of pool tokens provided for the query
params.sender: The sender passed to the operation. It can influence results (e.g., with user-dependent hooks)
params.unwrapWrapped: Flags indicating whether the corresponding token should be unwrapped or used as an ERC20
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the query

returns.amountsOut: Expected amounts of tokens to receive

params.exactBptAmountIn: The exact amount of `parentPool` tokens provided
params.parentPool: The address of the parent pool (which contains BPTs of other pools)
params.sender: The sender passed to the operation. It can influence results (e.g., with user-dependent hooks)
params.tokensOut: An array with all tokens from the child pools, and all non-BPT parent tokens, in arbitrary order
params.tokensToUnwrap: A list of ERC4626 tokens which should be unwrapped if encountered during pool traversal
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the operation

returns.amountsOut: An array with the expected amountOut of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensOut

removeLiquidityProportionalFromERC4626Po.details: An "ERC4626 pool" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI).

params.exactBptAmountIn: Exact amount of pool tokens provided
params.minAmountsOut: Minimum amounts of each token, sorted in token registration order
params.unwrapWrapped: Flags indicating whether the corresponding token should be unwrapped or used as an ERC20
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for removing liquidity
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH

removeLiquidityProportionalNestedPool(ad.details: A nested pool is one in which one or more tokens are BPTs from another pool (child pool). Since there are multiple pools involved, the token order is not well-defined, and must be specified by the caller. If the parent or nested pools contain ERC4626 tokens that appear in the `tokensToUnwrap` list, they will be unwrapped and their underlying tokens sent to the output. Otherwise, they will be treated as regular tokens.

params.exactBptAmountIn: The exact amount of `parentPool` tokens provided
params.minAmountsOut: An array with the minimum amountOut of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensOut
params.parentPool: The address of the parent pool (which contains BPTs of other pools)
params.tokensOut: An array with all tokens from the child pools, and all non-BPT parent tokens, in arbitrary order
params.tokensToUnwrap: A list of ERC4626 tokens which should be unwrapped if encountered during pool traversal
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the operation
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH

returns.amountsOut: An array with the actual amountOut of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensOut

addLiquidityProportionalToERC4626Pool(ad.notice: Add proportional amounts of tokens to an ERC4626 pool through the buffer.

addLiquidityUnbalancedNestedPool(address.notice: Adds liquidity unbalanced to a nested pool.

addLiquidityUnbalancedToERC4626Pool(addr.notice: Add arbitrary amounts of tokens to an ERC4626 pool through the buffer.

queryAddLiquidityProportionalToERC4626Po.notice: Queries an `addLiquidityProportionalToERC4626Pool` operation without actually executing it.

queryAddLiquidityUnbalancedNestedPool(ad.notice: Queries an `addLiquidityUnbalancedNestedPool` operation without actually executing it.

queryAddLiquidityUnbalancedToERC4626Pool.notice: Queries an `addLiquidityUnbalancedToERC4626Pool` operation without actually executing it.

queryRemoveLiquidityProportionalFromERC4.notice: Queries a `removeLiquidityProportionalFromERC4626Pool` operation without actually executing it.

queryRemoveLiquidityProportionalNestedPo.notice: Queries an `removeLiquidityProportionalNestedPool` operation without actually executing it.

removeLiquidityProportionalFromERC4626Po.notice: Remove proportional amounts of tokens from an ERC4626 pool, burning an exact pool token amount.

removeLiquidityProportionalNestedPool(ad.notice: Removes liquidity from a nested pool.
